---
title: "Architecture Comparison"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

::: {.compare-page}
::: {.compare-hero}
<span class="version-label">training and architecture axis</span>

# Model Intercomparison

This page compares the models where the **training objective, sampling strategy, physical constraint or architecture**
changed. It intentionally excludes pure inference sweeps such as solver, step count, sigma and tile overlap.
:::

## EKE Spectral View

The final version will use one LIR-generated plot with all EKE spectra on the same axes. For now, the figure below is
a placeholder so we can validate the layout before synchronizing real diagnostics.

![Placeholder EKE intercomparison across OceanLens training variants.](assets/figures/compare/eke_intercomparison_placeholder.svg){.wide-figure}

## Benchmark Matrix

::: {.metric-disclaimer}
The values below are placeholders. They are here to design the table and will be replaced by metrics computed from LIR
outputs on the same 2004 test day and domain.
:::

::: {.scoreboard}
| Model | Change | RMSE ↓ | Bias ↓ | Std ratio ↑ | EKE ↑ | Status |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| v8a DiT | pixel transformer | <span class="score good">0.71</span> | <span class="score good">0.03</span> | <span class="score good">0.82</span> | <span class="score good">0.86</span> | <span class="badge watch">promising, artifact check</span> |
| v7a Swin | local window attention | <span class="score good">0.74</span> | <span class="score good">0.04</span> | <span class="score good">0.78</span> | <span class="score good">0.83</span> | <span class="badge good">strong candidate</span> |
| Recoarsen FT | LR consistency | <span class="score mid">0.79</span> | <span class="score good">0.02</span> | <span class="score mid">0.70</span> | <span class="score mid">0.76</span> | <span class="badge good">physical guardrail</span> |
| Regional FT | weighted patches | <span class="score mid">0.81</span> | <span class="score mid">0.05</span> | <span class="score mid">0.72</span> | <span class="score mid">0.74</span> | <span class="badge watch">regional repair</span> |
| CFG FT | condition dropout | <span class="score mid">0.84</span> | <span class="score mid">0.06</span> | <span class="score good">0.76</span> | <span class="score mid">0.72</span> | <span class="badge watch">needs scale tuning</span> |
| v4 S1 | OT + logit-normal t | <span class="score mid">0.88</span> | <span class="score good">0.04</span> | <span class="score low">0.62</span> | <span class="score low">0.66</span> | <span class="badge base">baseline</span> |
| CNO only | deterministic mean | <span class="score low">0.93</span> | <span class="score good">0.03</span> | <span class="score low">0.42</span> | <span class="score low">0.51</span> | <span class="badge base">too smooth</span> |
| LR input | coarse reference | <span class="score low">1.18</span> | <span class="score low">0.08</span> | <span class="score low">0.18</span> | <span class="score low">0.22</span> | <span class="badge base">reference</span> |
:::

## What This Table Should Answer

::: {.comparison-grid}
::: {.comparison-card}
### Accuracy

Does the model reduce RMSE against HR without simply smoothing the residual field?
:::

::: {.comparison-card}
### Energy

Does the generated field recover the HR EKE spectrum, especially at the mesoscale and submesoscale frequencies?
:::

::: {.comparison-card}
### Physical consistency

Does the generated HR field remain compatible with the LR field after re-coarsening?
:::

::: {.comparison-card}
### Regional robustness

Does the model behave better in difficult regions such as the Mediterranean, Black Sea, Arctic and energetic western
boundary currents?
:::
:::

## Replacement Plan

1. Pull the finished `.npz`, metrics CSV and TensorBoard curves from LIR.
2. Generate one EKE spectrum figure for `thetao`, with HR, LR, CNO and all architecture variants.
3. Replace placeholder metrics with measured RMSE, bias, residual std ratio, EKE score and re-coarsening error.
4. Keep inference-only sweeps in version pages, not in this architecture table.
:::
